title: fix(brew): host-Brewfile fallback + secure temp file in sync/dump
description:
internal/brew/brew.go. Two defects in Sync():
1) Hardcoded temp path '/tmp/.Brewfile.current' (brew.go:16 dump target, :20 diff target). Concurrent 'bs brew sync' runs clobber each other. audit.go:135 already uses os.CreateTemp — copy that.
2) 'out, _ := exec.Run("diff", ...)' (brew.go:20) ignores error. diff exits 1 both on real diff AND on missing file, so a failed temp dump silently reports 'no drift'. Validate file exists / distinguish exit codes.
3) Correctness: cmd/brew/sync.go:16 and dump hardcode dotfiles/.Brewfile, ignoring host override. CLAUDE.md contract: prefer hosts/<machine>/.Brewfile, fall back to shared. Only audit implements this (audit.go:126-129). Extract that host-detection into shared helper, use in sync+dump+audit.
acceptance_criteria: brew sync uses os.CreateTemp; diff missing-file no longer reported as no-drift; sync/dump honor host Brewfile when present
close_reason: Implemented: BrewfilePath() shared helper, os.CreateTemp in Sync(), diff exit-code handling, sync/dump/audit all use host-aware Brewfile path. All tests pass.

title: Deep dive code quality review and simplification of bs CLI
description:
The bs CLI has accumulated layered fixes that may be degrading AI maintainability. Conduct a thorough code quality pass to simplify the implementation without changing observable behavior.

## Why this exists
Fixes stacking on fixes in the CLI source (cli/) have likely introduced complexity drift — duplicated logic, defensive code that's no longer needed, abstractions that don't pull their weight. This makes it harder for AI agents (and humans) to reason about and safely modify the code.

## What needs to be done
- Audit all commands in cli/ for structural complexity, redundant logic, and unclear control flow
- Identify and remove dead code, unnecessary guards, and over-engineered abstractions
- Flatten or consolidate patterns that are harder to follow than they need to be
- Ensure each command/function does one clear thing
- Do NOT change any observable behavior — same flags, same output, same error handling
- Run `make install` and smoke-test `bs help`, `bs audit`, `bs brew sync` after changes

## Acceptance criteria
- All existing bs commands behave identically before and after
- `make install` succeeds with no errors
- Code is materially simpler: fewer lines, less nesting, no duplicate logic
- A reviewer unfamiliar with the codebase can follow any command's logic without backtracking

title: refactor: unify tmux/cmux behind internal/workspace abstraction
description: Highest-leverage extensibility win. internal/tmux + internal/cmux (and cmd/tmux + cmd/cmux) are ~35-40% duplicated parallel impls of the same concept: config load (LoadSessions/LoadWorkspaces), 3-pane layout, agent staging, expandHome, coalesce, AllowedAgents (defined identically in tmux.go:9 and cmux.go:9), reset semantics. Every new workspace feature requires 4 edits across both. Introduce internal/workspace with a shared Workspace interface (Add/Start/Reset) + tmux and cmux backends; move AllowedAgents + shared helpers there. Sizes: internal/tmux/session.go=328, internal/cmux/start.go=358, internal/cmux/start_test.go=430, internal/tmux/session_test.go=481 — substantial, do incrementally and keep tests green. Depends on internal/exec + internal/paths landing first.
acceptance_criteria: single source for layout/agent/config logic; tmux+cmux are thin backends; existing behavior + tests preserved
close_reason: Unified tmux/cmux behind internal/workspace: shared agent registry, path helpers, LoadDir loader, and Start/Reset orchestration via Backend interface. Tests preserved + new workspace pkg tests. Merged to main, pushed.

title: fix(audit): slice bounds-check + symlink resolution
description:
internal/audit/audit.go.
1) audit.go:93 'rel := src[len(prefix)+1:]' panics if len(src) <= len(prefix) (e.g. WalkDir yields root or short path). Add bounds guard before slice.
2) audit.go:110 'if link == src' compares os.Readlink output to absolute src by exact string. Relative symlinks (../../bootstrap/...) yield false FOREIGN. Resolve both with filepath.EvalSymlinks or make absolute before compare.
acceptance_criteria: no panic on edge paths; relative symlinks classified OK not FOREIGN; add table test for both
close_reason: bounds-guard rel slice + linkMatches resolves relative symlinks; table tests added; all tests pass

title: fix(claude): plugin LatestVersion always empty makes upgrade unactionable
description: internal/pluginupgrade/plugins.go:17 LatestVersion() returns ('',nil) unconditionally. Upgrade UI shows 'current -> ?' for every plugin so user cannot tell if upgrade exists. Either implement real latest-version fetch, or remove the 'Latest' column and the unused function. plugins_test.go does not cover this.
acceptance_criteria: either Latest column shows real data or is removed; no misleading '?' output
close_reason: Removed unactionable Latest column + LatestVersion; table shows VERSION+STATUS, no misleading '?'

title: Harden bs CLI: bugs, correctness, structure, testability
description: Umbrella for findings from a 5-axis review of the bs CLI (cli/ dir, ~5.4k LOC Go/Cobra). Axes: testability, extensibility, accuracy, clarity, bugs. Baseline at review time: go vet clean, all 9 internal/ packages pass tests; cmd/* and internal/version have no tests (thin wrappers). Architecture: cmd/ = thin Cobra RunE wrappers; internal/ = logic; Executor interface injection used widely. Child issues carry file:line + fix so no re-research needed.
close_reason: All 9 child issues complete

title: Fix make install-plugins failures (caveman marketplace, stale team-update-report)
description: make install-plugins failed for two enabled plugins. caveman@caveman: caveman marketplace declared in dotfiles/.claude/settings.json extraKnownMarketplaces but missing from host settings.json (which fully overrides via whole-file symlink), so marketplace never registers. team-update-report@inmarket-skills: plugin un-merged from ThinkNear/agent-skills marketplace, stale reference. Fix: add caveman to host extraKnownMarketplaces; remove team-update-report from host enabledPlugins.
close_reason: Fixed: registered caveman marketplace in host settings, removed un-merged team-update-report, deduped extraKnownMarketplaces. make install-plugins now clean.

Architecture: registry-based Go package cli/internal/toolupgrade with mockable Executor/Tool interfaces. Implements plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-05-bs-tool-upgrade.md

title: fix bs cmux reset: workspace panes and commands not routing correctly
description:
bs cmux reset creates workspaces with correct layout/sizing but commands are not sent to the right panes. Three attempted fixes all failed in production: (1) send --surface pane:N silently dropped (pane refs are not valid surface IDs), (2) focus-pane before send is async/UI-only and doesn't affect routing, (3) list-pane-surfaces + send --surface surface:N approach — may still have ordering or timing issues. Workspaces are created, but pane contents are wrong or empty.

## Context
createWorkspaceFromConfig in cli/internal/cmux/start.go, Add in cli/internal/cmux/cmux.go. cmux is manaflow-ai Ghostty multiplexer. Key unknown: what does list-pane-surfaces actually return in production? Need to add logging or test manually with a real cmux session to observe actual pane/surface IDs.
acceptance_criteria: bs cmux reset creates all configured workspaces with: correct 3-pane layout, correct sizing (60% agent pane on left), correct commands in each pane (agent cmd staged in left, ls/bd ready in top-right, lazygit in bottom-right)
close_reason: Closed

title: Revisit auto-compaction token amount (autoCompactWindow), may be too low
description: autoCompactWindow is set to 180000 in hosts/MB-HYYXP4CRFJ/.claude/settings.json:241 (CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE=90 in env). Suspect the compaction trigger fires too early, compacting context sooner than needed on the 1M-context Opus model. Evaluate raising autoCompactWindow (and/or adjusting the pct override) to a value that better fits available context window.
acceptance_criteria: autoCompactWindow value reviewed against actual context window; adjusted to a justified value (or confirmed correct) in dotfiles and/or host settings.json.

title: Update Claude status line: drop session duration, show weekly limit reset
description: ccstatusline (configured via hosts/MB-HYYXP4CRFJ/.claude/settings.json:197 statusLine -> ~/.local/share/mise/shims/ccstatusline) currently shows session duration, which is no longer useful. Replace/remove that widget and add visibility into when the weekly session usage limit resets, so remaining quota before reset is clear at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Session duration removed from status line; weekly session limit reset time (or time-until-reset) is displayed; change applied to ccstatusline config and reflected on MB host.

title: Unify stdout vs stderr usage across bs CLI commands
description: bs-290 noted inconsistent stream usage: most command output goes to stdout via fmt.Print while sync.go errors go to os.Stderr. Audit all commands and route human-facing results to stdout, diagnostics/errors to stderr, consistently. Out of bs-290 scope (not in its acceptance criteria; has piping implications).
close_reason: Routed diagnostics/progress/prompts to stderr, kept results+confirmations on stdout across sync, version, cmux clear

title: docs(clarity): fix help text, naming, stale comments
description:
Code clarity: rename internal/tmux/tmux.go:84 resolveWindowName -> ensureUniqueWindowName; add docstring to internal/cmux/start.go:269-302 buildLayout (split-fraction direction flip is unreadable); fix stale comment in cmd/tmux/workspace.go (says 'git pull + bd ready', code does 'ls -al && bd ready').
User clarity: root Long (cmd/root.go:12-18) does not say what bs is / who it's for — add one-liner. Naming collision: 'bs sync' (mise+brew+plugins) vs 'bs brew sync' (show drift) — rename brew sync -> brew diff. --agent help hardcodes agent list, drifts from AllowedAgents — generate from source. --override worded 'kill' (tmux) vs 'close' (cmux) for same behavior — unify. Inconsistent stdout vs stderr usage across commands.
acceptance_criteria: help accurately describes bs + each subcommand; no stale comments; consistent verbs; agent list generated not hardcoded
close_reason: Renamed resolveWindowName->ensureUniqueWindowName, brew sync->brew diff; documented buildLayout flip; fixed stale tmux comment; root Long now explains bs; --agent list generated from AllowedAgents; --override wording unified across tmux/cmux.

title: test: add env/host/fs seams + extract RunE helpers for cmd coverage
description: Testability gaps (Executor injection already good & widely used). Hardcoded calls block tests: os.Getenv in internal/git/git.go:18 (RepoPath untestable), os.Hostname in internal/audit/audit.go:29, os.UserHomeDir in internal/sync/sync.go:69 + cmd/sync.go:24, os.CreateTemp in audit.go:135 (checkBrew untestable). Add EnvGetter + Hostname seams. Separately, all cmd/* logic lives inside Cobra RunE closures (0% coverage) — extract runVersion(ex)/runSync(ex)/runAudit(ex) helpers so logic is unit-testable apart from Cobra. Pairs with internal/exec + internal/paths refactor.
acceptance_criteria: RepoPath/audit Run testable without touching real env/host/tmp; at least version+sync+audit have extracted testable run funcs with tests
close_reason: Added EnvGetter/Hostname/Home/TempDir seams + extracted runVersion/runSync/runAudit; cmd 0→84%, audit 17→79%, git 73→100% coverage

title: refactor: centralize internal/exec (executors) + internal/paths
description:
Foundation refactor that unblocks the tmux/cmux unification and testability work.
1) internal/exec: 7 near-identical realExecutor copies live in cmd/version.go:48, cmd/brew/brew.go:27, cmd/tmux/tmux.go:27, cmd/cmux/cmux.go:28 (cmux strips env), cmd/tool/tool.go:24 (adds LookPath), plus streamingExecutor in cmd/version.go:57. Each internal/* also redeclares the Executor interface (8 copies). Define canonical Executor + LookPathExecutor + Real/Streaming/CMux impls once in internal/exec; import everywhere. Single place for future logging/timeout/tracing.
2) internal/paths: config paths duplicated verbatim across start+reset for both tools — .config/tmux/sessions (cmd/tmux/start.go:43, reset.go:32), .config/cmux/workspaces (cmd/cmux/start.go:47, reset.go:35), .claude/settings.json (cmd/sync.go:28). No env override except $BOOTSTRAP_REPO. Add internal/paths.Load() resolving env-first then defaults.
acceptance_criteria: one Executor source; one paths source; cmd packages import them; tests green
close_reason: Centralized executors into internal/exec (Executor/LookPathExecutor + Real/Streaming/CMux) and config paths into internal/paths.Load(); all cmd+internal packages import them; build, vet, tests green; merged to main and pushed.

title: fix(cmux): expandHome silent fallback + brittle 'OK ' parse
description:
internal/cmux/start.go.
1) expandHome (start.go:350-358): if os.UserHomeDir() fails, returns literal '~/...' which cmux then receives unexpanded. Return error or warn instead of silently passing bad path.
2) start.go:229 'wsID := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(wsOut), "OK ")' — if cmux ever returns bare 'OK' (no ref), TrimPrefix leaves 'OK' as the id, breaking all downstream cmux calls. Not a live bug today (cmux emits 'OK <ref>') but defensive: use SplitN and validate len.
Related parsing fragility (lower urgency): listPaneIDs/listSurfaceIDsForPanes (start.go:307-348) assume cmux column layout. Per project CLAUDE.md, always confirm against https://cmux.com/docs/api before touching cmux parsing.
acceptance_criteria: expandHome surfaces error on home lookup failure; OK-parse validates ref non-empty
close_reason: expandHome errors on home-lookup failure; parseWorkspaceRef validates non-empty ref in start.go+cmux.go; tests added

title: fix(cli): surface swallowed errors + unchecked writes
description:
Low-sev error hygiene.
1) cmd/version.go:34 'dirty, _ := igit.IsDirty()' swallows error (git.go itself is correct, returns (false,err)). Surface or log it. version.go:31 error path just prints and continues — consider clearer message + actionable hint (git installed? $BOOTSTRAP_REPO set?).
2) Unchecked output writes that hide partial failures: internal/pluginupgrade/pluginupgrade.go:111,120,127,129,133,136 (fmt.Fprint*); internal/toolupgrade/toolupgrade.go:154 (tw.Flush). Check/return on critical paths.
acceptance_criteria: version dirty error not silently dropped; flush/write errors checked on report paths
close_reason: Surfaced IsDirty error in version cmd; wrapped report output in writeutil.ErrWriter so write/flush errors are checked on report paths in plugin/tool upgrade. Added writeutil pkg + tests.

title: Simplify and unify machine bootstrap installation steps
description: The install process has grown into multiple disconnected steps that are easy to forget or run out of order. Currently requires: make install (symlinks), mise install (tool versions), make brew-install (packages), and now make install-plugins (Claude plugins) — each a separate manual step with implicit ordering dependencies. Need a brainstorming session to design a unified, memorable installation flow, possibly via a single make target or a bs CLI command that orchestrates the full sequence.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reconcile gcloud SDK path mismatch (manual install vs brew cask)
description: dotfiles/.zsh/gcloud.zsh sources $HOME/google-cloud-sdk/path.zsh.inc and completion.zsh.inc (a manual SDK install layout), but dotfiles/.Brewfile installs 'cask "gcloud-cli"', which installs to a different location. The sourced files likely don't exist, so gcloud PATH/completions may not load as intended.
acceptance_criteria: gcloud.zsh sources the correct path for the brew cask install (or the install method is made consistent); gcloud + completions load correctly in a fresh shell

title: Remove vestigial pyenv install and leftover PATH entry
description: pyenv is installed via brew (/opt/homebrew/bin/pyenv) but is NOT in dotfiles/.Brewfile (shows as brew drift in 'bs audit'/'bs brew sync'). dotfiles/.zsh/pyenv.zsh already states 'python is managed by mise; pyenv no longer used', yet dotfiles/.zsh/0_path.zsh still has an active 'pathPrefix "$HOME/.pyenv/bin"  # pyenv' line.
acceptance_criteria: pyenv uninstalled (or removed from PATH); active $HOME/.pyenv/bin pathPrefix line in 0_path.zsh removed; bs audit shows no pyenv drift

title: implement bs cmux add
description:
Implement bs cmux add --cwd <path> [--name <name>] [--agent <agent>] command in the bs CLI, analogous to bs tmux add.

What needs to be done:
- Add cli/cmd/cmux/ package with cmux.go (parent command), workspace.go (add subcommand)
- Add cli/internal/cmux/ package with Add() function and Executor interface
- Wire into cli/cmd/root.go

Layout to create:
  Left pane  : agent command staged, no Enter — claude staged as 'claude agents --cwd <cwd>'
  Top-right  : ls -al && bd ready (executed)
  Bottom-right: lazygit (executed)

Implementation approach:
  1. cmux new-workspace (creates workspace, captures CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID if available)
  2. cmux new-split right (creates right column; active surface is now right)
  3. cmux send "ls -al && bd ready" then cmux send-key enter (top-right)
  4. cmux new-split down (splits right column; active surface is now bottom-right)
  5. cmux send "lazygit" then cmux send-key enter
  6. Focus left pane via cmux list-surfaces --json + cmux focus-surface, then cmux send "claude agents --cwd <cwd>" (no enter)

CWD must be passed to all panes; cmux does not have a per-split -c flag like tmux, so cd into the directory as the first command in each pane, or rely on the shell's inherited CWD if the workspace opens in the right directory (needs testing).

Allowed agents: claude, codex, gemini, opencode, pi (same as tmux version). Only claude gets the 'agents --cwd <cwd>' expansion.

Acceptance criteria:
- bs cmux add --cwd ~/code/myproject opens a workspace with the 3-pane layout
- All panes are cwd'd to the provided path
- Claude is staged as 'claude agents --cwd <path>' without executing
- Other agents are staged as their bare name without executing
- --name overrides the workspace label if cmux supports naming
- Requires cmux to be installed; errors clearly if not found
close_reason: Implemented cli/cmd/cmux and cli/internal/cmux packages with bs cmux add command matching bs tmux add layout

title: bs cmux: workspace management commands
description: Add a bs cmux command group that provides cmux-based workspace management analogous to the existing bs tmux commands. cmux is a macOS-native terminal built on Ghostty for managing multiple AI coding agents. It exposes a socket-based CLI for workspace, split, surface, and status control. This epic covers implementing add, start, and reset subcommands plus personal cmux config tracking.
close_reason: Closed

title: tmux session-closed hook: auto-clear claude-pending flag on session death
description:
## Why
Flag files in ~/.local/share/claude-pending/ persist when a tmux session is killed or the machine restarts. Without a cleanup hook, stale entries accumulate for sessions that no longer exist.

## What needs to be done
- Add a session-closed hook to tmux.conf that deletes the flag file for the dying session:
  set-hook -g session-closed 'run-shell "rm -f ~/.local/share/claude-pending/#{session_name}"'
- This handles kill-session, exit, and any other session teardown path
- Does not cover reboots (tmux doesn't fire hooks on shutdown) — stale reboot entries are handled by prune-on-open in the popup picker (bs-ad5)

## Acceptance criteria
- Killing a tmux session removes its flag file
- Surviving sessions are unaffected
- Pairs with bs-ad5 prune-on-open for full staleness coverage

## Parent epic
Part of bs-en9: Epic: tmux Claude pending notification system
close_reason: Added session-closed hook to tmux.conf

title: Epic: tmux Claude pending notification system
description:
Tracks the full system for surfacing which tmux sessions have a Claude agent waiting for attention.

## Why
macOS notifications are ephemeral. When running multiple concurrent Claude sessions, there's no persistent way to know which sessions have stopped and need a response. This epic delivers two complementary UIs on top of a shared state layer.

## Deliverables
- State layer: flag files written by claude-notify, cleared on navigation
- Popup picker: fzf popup (prefix+N) with live pane preview for browsing pending sessions
- Status bar: second tmux status line showing pending sessions above the existing Dracula bar

title: claude-notify state layer: write and clear per-session flag files
description:
Shared foundation for the tmux pending-notification features. Extends claude-notify to persist session state to disk and clears it on navigation.

## Why
Both the popup picker and the two-status-line indicator need to know which sessions have pending Claude notifications. This state must survive across tmux sessions and be readable by shell scripts. A flag-file-per-session approach is simple, low-latency, and requires no daemon.

## What needs to be done

### Write flags (in claude-notify)
- On Stop or Notification hook fire, write a file to ~/.local/share/claude-pending/<session_name>
- File contents (one field per line or JSON): project name (cwd_project), tmux target (session:window.pane), timestamp (epoch seconds)
- Create the directory if it doesn't exist

### Clear flags (tmux hooks)
- Add an 'after-select-window' hook in tmux.conf that deletes the flag file for the current session when you switch to it
- This ensures the indicator clears automatically when you actually visit the session

### Helpers (optional but useful)
- A small helper function or script that lists pending sessions (reads all flag files, emits formatted output) — shared by both consumers rather than duplicated

## Acceptance criteria
- claude-notify writes a flag file when Stop/Notification fires
- Flag file is deleted when the user switches to that session's window
- Flag directory is created automatically if missing
- Works correctly with multiple concurrent Claude sessions
acceptance_criteria: flag written on hook fire; flag cleared on window switch; multi-session works correctly
close_reason: flag write/clear and helper script implemented and pushed

title: Two tmux status lines: pending notifications on top, Dracula on bottom
description:
Add a second tmux status line so pending Claude sessions are always visible without displacing the existing Dracula status bar.

## Why
The current single-line Dracula status bar has no room for a persistent pending-session indicator without fighting Dracula's ownership of status-right. A second status line sidesteps that entirely and gives full layout control.

## Layout preference
- Status line 1 (top row): pending Claude sessions — blank/invisible when nothing is waiting, shows session names with a visual indicator (e.g. ⧖) when sessions need attention
- Status line 2 (bottom row): existing Dracula output, completely untouched

This order keeps Dracula at the bottom where it currently lives (muscle memory preserved) and adds the notification layer above it.

## What needs to be done

### State layer (shared with popup bead)
- Extend ~/.local/bin/claude-notify to write flag files under ~/.local/share/claude-pending/ (same state layer as the popup feature — both features share this)
- Clear flags on session/window navigation via tmux hooks

### Status line configuration
- Set 'set -g status 2' in dotfiles/.config/tmux/tmux.conf (requires tmux 3.2+, verify first)
- status-format[1] = top row: a #() segment that reads pending flag files and emits session names, or empty string when none pending
- status-format[0] = bottom row: leave as Dracula's output — do not manually set, let Dracula own it
- Style the top row: muted/invisible background when empty (zero visual noise); highlighted (e.g. amber) when sessions are pending

## Visual behavior
Nothing pending — top row is blank, effectively invisible:
  bootstrap  1:zsh  2:nvim  3:claude       ✓ main  ⚡ 42%  CPU 12%  RAM 4.2G  14:32

Sessions pending — top row appears above Dracula:
  ⧖ api-service · bootstrap · infra
  bootstrap  1:zsh  2:nvim  3:claude       ✓ main  ⚡ 42%  CPU 12%  RAM 4.2G  14:32

## Acceptance criteria
- 'set -g status 2' confirmed working on installed tmux version
- Top row invisible when no pending sessions
- Top row shows session names when Claude has stopped and is waiting
- Bottom row (Dracula) unchanged in appearance and behavior
- Pending state clears when session is visited
acceptance_criteria: top row invisible when idle; shows pending session names when waiting; Dracula bottom row unchanged; clears on navigation

title: tmux popup for browsing pending Claude notifications
description:
Add a tmux keybinding (prefix + N) that opens a display-popup with an fzf list of sessions where Claude has stopped and is awaiting attention.

## Why
macOS notifications are ephemeral — they disappear before you get to them, leaving no way to see which sessions still need a response. This popup provides a persistent, navigable queue.

## What needs to be done

### Popup script (~/.local/bin/claude-pending-picker)
- On open: prune stale flag files whose session name doesn't appear in `tmux ls` — handles reboot orphans silently
- Read remaining flag files and format for fzf: project name, tmux target, elapsed time (e.g. '14 min ago')
- fzf preview pane: show last N lines of that pane's scrollback via `tmux capture-pane -t <target> -p -e`
- On selection (Enter): switch to the session (`tmux switch-client -t <target>`) and delete its flag file
- Keybind d (dismiss selected): delete the flag file for the focused entry without navigating; reload list
- Keybind D (dismiss all): delete all flag files and close popup
- On abort (Esc/q): dismiss without side effects
- Empty state: show 'no pending sessions' message when nothing is waiting

### tmux integration
- Bind prefix + N to `display-popup -E -w 80% -h 60% ~/.local/bin/claude-pending-picker`
- Add to dotfiles/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

## Acceptance criteria
- Pressing prefix+N shows all pending sessions with elapsed time
- Moving cursor in fzf shows live pane preview for that session
- Selecting an entry navigates directly to that session and removes it from the list
- Pressing d dismisses the focused entry (removes flag) without navigating
- Pressing D clears all pending flags and closes the popup
- Pressing Esc dismisses without side effects
- Stale entries from reboots are pruned automatically on popup open
- Empty state handled gracefully
acceptance_criteria: prefix+N opens popup; fzf preview shows pane scrollback on cursor move; selection navigates and clears flag; empty state handled gracefully
close_reason: Closed

title: track personal cmux config changes
description:
Capture and version-control personal cmux configuration as changes are made to the setup.

cmux reads from:
  - ~/.config/ghostty/config  (terminal rendering — already tracked via dotfiles symlink)
  - ~/.config/cmux/cmux.json  (app settings — needs symlink)
  - .cmux/cmux.json            (project-scoped — per-repo, not tracked here)

What needs to be done:
- Add dotfiles/.config/cmux/cmux.json with personal preferences as they evolve
- Wire it into scripts/install.sh as a symlink (or verify make install covers ~/.config/ files automatically)
- Document notable config sections used: app, terminal, notifications, sidebar, automation, shortcuts

This is an ongoing / living bead — update it as new cmux config preferences are discovered and committed. Not a one-time implementation task.

Acceptance criteria:
- ~/.config/cmux/cmux.json is a symlink into the dotfiles repo
- Personal cmux preferences are committed and survive a fresh machine bootstrap
close_reason: cmux.json captured from plist, symlinked via make install, pushed

title: implement bs cmux reset
description:
Implement bs cmux reset command analogous to bs tmux reset.

Depends on bs cmux start being implemented first.

Proposed behavior:
  bs cmux reset
  - Closes all open cmux workspaces (list-workspaces --json + close-workspace --workspace <id> for each)
  - Then calls the same workspace-recreation logic as bs cmux start (no restore, clean slate)
  - Must be run from outside cmux or handle graceful self-close

Research questions:
1. Does cmux have a way to close all workspaces in one command, or does it require iterating?
2. What happens when the calling terminal is itself inside a cmux workspace that gets closed?
3. Is there a cmux equivalent to tmux kill-server?

Acceptance criteria (draft):
- bs cmux reset closes all workspaces and rebuilds from config
- Errors clearly if no workspaces are defined
- Handles the 'running inside cmux' case (warn or refuse, like tmux version)
close_reason: Closed

title: implement bs cmux start
description:
Implement bs cmux start command analogous to bs tmux start. This ticket requires research before implementation.

Research questions:
1. Does cmux have a native workspace config format (beyond .cmux/cmux.json) that defines workspace layouts declaratively?
2. Can cmux restore-session recreate workspace splits and surface commands, or only layout+dirs?
3. Is there a cmux analog to tmuxinator YAML for scripting workspace creation from config files?
4. How does cmux handle workspaces that already exist (idempotency)?

Proposed behavior (to refine after research):
  bs cmux start [--no-restore] [--override]
  - Reads workspace config files from ~/.config/cmux/workspaces/*.json (or YAML — TBD)
  - Optionally runs cmux restore-session before creating defined workspaces
  - --override: close existing workspace with same name before recreating
  - --no-restore: skip the restore-session step

Config format TBD based on research — either reuse the tmuxinator YAML schema or define a cmux-native JSON schema.

Acceptance criteria (draft):
- bs cmux start creates workspaces defined in config files
- Existing workspaces are left untouched unless --override is passed
- Restore step can be skipped with --no-restore
- Config format is documented
close_reason: implemented bs cmux start with --no-restore, --override, JSON workspace configs, local overlay dir, and restore-session integration
